title: The Lull and the Locked Missiles

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: A genuinely even day at 41: direct US-Iran fighting stayed paused into the weekend while the rhetoric turned to locked-and-loaded threats, a typhoon made landfall and an outbreak worsened — roughly cancelled by firm Friday markets, a 99%-accurate womb-cancer blood test, HIV-prevention shots scaling across Africa, and renewables meeting all of 2025's electricity growth.

style.genre: dusty trip-hop / neo-soul — a sampled vibraphone laying warm F# mixolydian chords over a laid-back RhythmAce kit with tape-echo, a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline in the gaps, sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's quiet wins, a warm sawtooth bass walking under a sine sub; mixolydian's lowered-seventh natural E keeping the warmth honest and just-off-level — a mixed day where real danger and real progress land even, neither rising nor sinking

pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.72).room(0.18),
  s("hh*8").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.3).pan(sine.range(0.35,0.65).slow(7)).delay(0.15).delaytime(0.18),
  s("~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~").bank("EmuSP12").gain(0.32).pan(0.4),
  note("<[fs3,as3,cs4] [e3,gs3,b3] [b2,ds3,fs3] [cs3,e3,gs3]>").s("vibraphone").attack(0.02).release(0.7).gain(0.5).room(0.45).rsize(3).lpf(sine.range(1400,2800).slow(12)),
  note("as4 ~ cs5 e5 ~ b4 ~ fs4 ~ gs4 ~ cs5 b4 ~ ~ ~").s("marimba").gain(0.44).room(0.32).delay(0.25).delaytime(0.28).pan(0.45).lpf(3200),
  note("~ ~ ~ ~ fs5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ cs6 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").s("glockenspiel").gain(0.24).room(0.5).delay(0.2),
  note("fs2 fs3 e2 e3 b1 b2 cs2 cs3").s("sawtooth").lpf(600).gain(0.42).attack(0.01).release(0.22),
  note("<fs1 e1 b1 cs2>").s("sine").gain(0.3).lpf(200).attack(0.05).release(0.9)
)
sampleSources: Strudel default library (VCSL vibraphone/marimba/glockenspiel, RhythmAce & EmuSP12 drum machines)

lines: Two days the guns stayed quiet, a lull we didn't trust
A thousand missiles named out loud, locked and loaded if they must
A festival in Toronto, thirteen thousand in the square
And a thirteen-year-old girl in Sumy who isn't anywhere
So we hold at forty-one, same as yesterday
A storm making landfall, a market that held its way
Count the columns twice, the loss against the light
Neither one wins the ledger, and neither loses it tonight
Bavi hit the coast at Yuhuan, two million moved inland
Black Hawks lifting camp kids off a river's rising hand
But a blood test found the cancer, ninety-nine of a hundred true
And the sun ran all the new demand — that's a column holding too
